query_review: I use these for my sandwiches and bento box lunches.  I like to cut patterns out of white and wheat bread and mix the colors of breads, and I also use these to cut various circles in cheeses, lunch meats and veggies, to make abstract art salads.  They also come in handy for decorating bento box art- because circles are often the basis for various designs I make.  You do need to keep these dry, so wipe them off carefully.  I saved some of those packets that dehydrate things (the kind you find in the bottom of the jerkey package, for example, that say DO NOT EAT)- and I put that in the tin with the cutters.  So far no rust, and I use these little guys constantly.  I especially like how deep these cut, as some cutters aren't quite deep enough for making sandwich art- and some thick breads are too deep to cut through.hmmm- it won't let me add a link.  So if you want to see the pictures of what this and other Amazon cutters can do, google "A year of lunches"- my sopressata roll up was the first link that came up for me.
